demos.thread:
From: office@strataops.example

--- TENANT
Subject: Fwd: HVAC issue — unit 14B
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: PM Office <office@strataops.example>
Date: Mon, Feb 9, 2026
Subject: Re: HVAC issue

Hi Stephen — thanks for your patience. Forwarding to the maintenance triage team so this gets actioned.

PM Office

> On Feb 7, 2026, Stephen Lim <stephen.lim@example.com> wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
> The central heating in unit 14B has stopped working entirely. We woke up to a cold apartment on Saturday morning and have been using portable heaters since. The thermostat appears functional (display lights up, setpoint adjusts) but no warm air comes from the vents in any room.
>
> We have a 2-year-old at home and the indoor temperature this morning was 12°C. Please advise.
>
> Best regards,
> Stephen Lim

From: diego.r@example.com

--- TENANT
Subject: unit 8A
Hi - issue in unit 8A's kitchen. Please send someone soon.

— Diego

From: priya.sharma@example.com

--- TENANT
Subject: Front-left stove burner not working
The front-left burner on my stove won't ignite — I can hear the clicker but no flame. Other three burners work fine. Not urgent, but would appreciate getting it fixed when you get a chance. Unit 9D, Riverside.

Regards,
Priya Sharma

From: alex.j@example.com

--- TENANT
Subject: key broke in lock again
3rd time this month my key has snapped off in the front door lock. clearly the lock is failing. im not locked out right now, im inside, but next time i leave im stuffed. need this replaced not just lubricated like last time. unit 7C

signature.instructions: Analyze the provided tenant maintenance email thread and classify it into the appropriate category (e.g., locksmith, appliance, hvac), determine the urgency level (low, medium, high), identify any risk flags (e.g., security risk, habitability violation), and confirm that it is indeed a maintenance request. Output the results in the specified JSON format.

fields.description: Full thread, oldest first, each turn labeled TENANT or AGENT.
The maintenance category. Null when not_a_maintenance_request is true.

From physical facts only, never tone. high = immediate threat to habitability/safety/property (active leak, no heat in cold, gas smell, locked out). medium = a real fault in daily use but not immediate (partly-broken appliance, slow drain). low = cosmetic/minor, or no concrete physical fact. Null when not_a_maintenance_request is true.
Only flags with an explicit physical signal in the body; opt-in, not implied by the category. Empty list when none apply.
True when the email is about something other than a fix the landlord owns (lease, invoice, dispute, parking, billing, owner message), or carries no maintenance signal at all.
Human queue to route a non-maintenance email to; null otherwise.
